response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, device orchestration tools) and well over the required 8 years of development experience. However, this is a people-leadership role requiring 2+ years of people management and 3+ years of technical leadership, and the resume shows no evidence of managing engineers, conducting performance reviews, or owning team roadmaps. His mobile experience is limited to a brief iOS/Apple TV stint, and there is no organizational-change or formal leadership track record, making him a poor fit for a Level II Manager position despite solid technical depth.
strengths: Authentic developer-tools background: built test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), CI servers, and device orchestration tooling
15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Demonstrated knowledge sharing via workshops, courses, and conference talks, plus open-source project ownership
concerns: No formal people management experience — no evidence of direct reports, 1:1s, or performance management
No demonstrated technical leadership title or scope (leading teams, setting roadmaps) in any role
Mobile-platform experience is shallow and dated (one iOS/Apple TV role in 2015-2016)

key_factors.factor: Developer tools and testing infrastructure expertise
Lack of formal technical leadership track record
